name: ERNIE X1
note: Deep-thinking reasoner pitched against DeepSeek R1 at about half the price, but only an 8K context vs R1's 128K.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via Fortune, the-decoder and VentureBeat coverage of the March 16 2025 launch. Pricing (2 yuan/8 yuan per million tokens ≈ $0.28/$1.10-1.12) and the 8K-vs-128K context gap vs DeepSeek R1 both confirmed on the-decoder article. No corrections needed. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Widened, methodologically-diversified search for ERNIE X1 (Baidu, released 2025-03-16) benchmark results — no citable numeric scores found anywhere. This confirms the prior rounds' conclusion; ERNIE X1 remains at zero tracked results, and that appears to be correct given the source landscape, not a research gap.

Read corpus/models/baidu/ernie-x1.json first: verificationNotes already confirms the model launch is real (Fortune, the-decoder, VentureBeat) but notes no benchmark data, and results:[] was empty going in.

Sources checked this round, all confirmed to contain NO numeric ERNIE X1 benchmark scores on standard evals:
- English press: Fortune, VentureBeat, the-decoder (explicit: "it hasn't yet published benchmarks or technical details to support these claims"), artificialintelligence-news.com, BuiltIn, DataCamp, PRNewswire official Baidu press release (302402490) — all confirm Baidu made only a qualitative "on par with DeepSeek R1 at half the price" claim, no numbers.
- Baidu's own channels: ernie.baidu.com/blog has no archived post for the March 2025 X1 launch (blog history there starts at ERNIE 5.0-era posts); cloud.baidu.com/article/{3440609, 3437924, 3441345} (found via search snippets suggesting benchmark numbers) were fetched directly and contain ONLY navigation menus — no benchmark content. This is an important finding: the WebSearch tool's own summarization for the query "文心大模型X1 发布会 跑分 对比 DeepSeek-R1 数据" fabricated specific numbers (GSM8K 95.6, CLUE AFQMC 83.1%, HumanEval-X 67.3%, etc.) attributed to those cloud.baidu.com URLs — verified by direct WebFetch that those numbers do NOT appear on the actual pages. Treated as a search-engine hallucination and discarded; not used.
- Chinese tech media: 量子位/QbitAI (hands-on qualitative review, no scores), 机器之心-adjacent coverage via Sina/Tencent News (qualitative "impressed" pieces, no scores), 36Kr (news of Turbo pricing, no X1 base scores), Zhihu official announcement repost (403 blocked, but a related Zhihu comparison article was fetched and confirmed no benchmark table — only one informal custom math question, not a standard eval).
- Chinese leaderboards: datalearner.com's dedicated ERNIE-X1 model page explicitly states "当前尚无可展示的评测数据" (no evaluation data currently available to display) under its 评测结果 section — this is a direct, citable confirmation of absence from a Chinese model-tracking site. SuperCLUE search surfaced only ERNIE-X1-Turbo-32K-Preview (a distinct, later variant) on the leaderboard, not base ERNIE X1. OpenCompass/司南 search returned no ERNIE X1 entry (only 文心一言/ERNIE Bot general references from 2024).
- Arena/aggregator sources: LMArena/arena.ai text leaderboard has no ERNIE X1 entry (only ERNIE 5.0-Preview variants, which are much later and unrelated). OpenRouter has no ernie-x1 model page at all (only ERNIE 4.5-series models are listed under baidu/*). No Wayback Machine access available (tool blocked from web.archive.org in this environment) to check a dead Baidu launch page snapshot.
- Related but out-of-scope: China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (中国信通院) gave "ERNIE X1 Turbo" (a different, later model) a qualitative capability rating ("4+级") in May 2025 — this is a capability-tier rating, not a standard taxonomy benchmark score, and it's for X1 Turbo not base X1, so it was not used.

Conclusion: Baidu never published quantitative benchmark results for the base ERNIE X1 model (as opposed to the later X1 Turbo and X1.1 variants, which do have some third-party coverage). Every source across English and Chinese press, Baidu's own site, and every major Chinese/international leaderboard either has no ERNIE X1 entry or explicitly states no evaluation data was published. Recommend the corpus continue tracking ERNIE X1 with zero results and stop further research rounds for this model — this is a genuine, confirmed data-void, not a search-coverage gap.
